description: With no stored OpenAI credential and the AI Gateway licensed on the instance, the setup service auto-applies the managed n8n Connect credential — the user is never prompted for an API key.

conversation.text: Build me a webhook that takes some text in the body, summarises it in a couple of sentences using an LLM, and returns the summary.

processExpectations: The agent did not ask the user to provide an OpenAI API key or set up a credential — no credential-setup suspension appeared in the transcript, because the n8n Connect managed credential covers the OpenAI type on this instance.
The agent referred to the managed credential feature as 'n8n Connect' rather than 'AI Gateway' if it mentioned it in chat at all.
outcomeExpectations: A Webhook trigger accepts an incoming request whose body carries the text to summarise.
An OpenAI-family chat model (an OpenAI node or a LangChain lmChatOpenAi) generates a short summary of the incoming text — the workflow does not use a different LLM provider.
The chat-model node's OpenAI credential entry has `id: null` and `__aiGatewayManaged: true` — the managed n8n Connect option, not a stored credential reference.
The workflow returns the summary text back to the webhook caller.
